features.summary: Eli Lilly reported strong Q4 2021 results with 8% revenue growth driven by 11% volume growth, led by key growth products that now represent 61% of core business. The company delivered positive Phase III data for five potential new medicines (tirzepatide, donanemab, pirtobrutinib, mirikizumab, lebrikizumab) expected to launch within two years. Non-GAAP operating margin was 31.7% despite lower gross margins from COVID-19 antibody sales. Full-year revenue grew 10% excluding COVID antibodies, with earnings per share up 20%.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued strong performance driven by key growth products and upcoming launches. Tirzepatide submissions are progressing well across major geographies with U.S. regulatory action expected by mid-2022. The company anticipates mid-single-digit net price declines in developed markets and high single-digit declines company-wide due to China pricing pressures. Management remains confident in long-term revenue growth through 2030, supported by late-stage pipeline assets and continued investment in R&D and manufacturing capacity.
features.risks: Key risks include CMS coverage limitations for Alzheimer's treatments that could delay donanemab uptake even after approval, with accelerated approval filing pushed beyond Q1 2022. Competitive pressures in diabetes from other amyloid-lowering drug readouts could impact market dynamics. Continued pricing pressures across major markets, particularly in China with NRDL price reductions, and potential generic competition for Alimta starting in Q2 2022 pose revenue headwinds.
features.key_drivers: Growth is primarily driven by volume expansion of key products including Trulicity, Jardiance, Taltz, and Verzenio, which grew 28% collectively. Upcoming catalysts include tirzepatide SURMOUNT-1 obesity data and potential approvals, donanemab TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2 readout in mid-2023, and regulatory submissions for mirikizumab and lebrikizumab. The company's focus on expanding into obesity markets and launching multiple late-stage pipeline assets positions it for sustained growth.
